dialogueChunks.intro: You won't believe what happened while you were gone, {player_name}. The competition just turned ugly.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: One of the rival crafters sabotaged the other's workshop. Tools ruined, materials contaminated. The victim is accusing the other of cheating, and the accused is furious at the accusation. {target_npc} tried to step in and got shouted out of the room. Now neither crafter will produce anything for {location} until they receive a public apology from the other. Meanwhile, the settlement is running low on {quest_item} because both workshops have shut down. Someone needs to gather emergency supplies from the quarry {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: We can't wait for their egos to deflate. The settlement needs supplies now, regardless of who wronged whom.

playerResponses.text: Emergency supplies first, bruised egos second. I'll handle the quarry.
Sabotage over a craft rivalry? These two deserve each other. But the settlement doesn't deserve to suffer.
Let them stew in their own mess. I'm not cleaning up after petty feuds.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the rivalry has spilled outside {location} and now other people are caught in the crossfire.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Both competitors sent apprentices to the same supply source {location_hint} at the same time. Instead of sharing, the apprentices fought over it. Now both groups are stranded out there: injured, out of supplies, and surrounded by {enemy_type} that moved in during the commotion. {target_npc} says neither master crafter will send a rescue party because they refuse to be the one who admits they need help. Someone has to bring those apprentices back before {quest_stakes}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Innocent people stuck between stubborn masters and hungry monsters. They need you, {player_name}.

playerResponses.text: Apprentices shouldn't suffer for their masters' pride. I'll bring them home.
A rescue mission because of a craft dispute. Absurd. But I won't let them die for it.
Their masters sent them. Their masters can rescue them.

dialogueChunks.intro: I thought getting the materials would calm them down, {player_name}. I was wrong.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Both crafters used the {quest_item} you brought to build their competing prototypes, and now they're demanding a public trial to determine the winner. The problem is, each side has bribed half the judges. {target_npc} found out and canceled the trial, which only made both sides angrier. Now one of them has threatened to take their skills and leave {location} entirely. We need a third source of {quest_item} to build a neutral prototype that neither side can dispute: something from the old foundry {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: An impartial comparison might be the only way to end this. But getting materials from that foundry won't be easy.

playerResponses.text: A neutral test sounds fair. I'll get the materials from the foundry.
Bribed judges? This rivalry has rotted both of them. But I'll play along.
I'm done enabling this contest. Let them figure it out.

dialogueChunks.intro: The situation just escalated, {player_name}. The rivalry has attracted outside attention.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Word of the competition reached a merchant guild in a neighboring region. They've sent a representative to {location} offering to buy out both workshops and install their own people. If either crafter accepts, {location} loses its independence. {target_npc} is panicking: the guild representative has been meeting with both crafters privately, playing them against each other. Someone needs to find out what the guild is really offering: their records are being kept at a camp {location_hint}, guarded by hired muscle and {enemy_type}.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we can expose the guild's true terms, maybe both crafters will realize {quest_threat} and finally stop fighting each other.

playerResponses.text: An outside guild exploiting a local feud? I'll get those records.
Tamed {enemy_type} and corporate scheming. This keeps getting stranger.
Guild politics are above my pay grade. Handle it yourselves.
